description:
You are a data-scientist who has just received a small event log and you must quickly determine how many times every distinct event type occurs.  
    1.  Initial data set  
        • One CSV file already exists at  
          /home/user/data/raw/events.csv  
        • The file contains a single header line followed by exactly 21 data lines (22 lines total).  
        • The four columns are: id,user,event_type,timestamp  
        • Column separator: the literal comma ( , ).  
        • The data lines contain these exact values for the third column (event_type) and in this exact distribution:  
              view      6 times  
              login     5 times  
              purchase  4 times  
              logout    3 times  
              click     2 times  
              unknown   1 time  

    2.  Your tasks  
        a. Create the directory /home/user/data/cleaned (if it does not yet exist).  
        b. From the CSV, compute the frequency of every distinct event_type (column 3).  
           • Ignore the header line entirely.  
           • Count must be case-sensitive (the file only contains lower-case words).  
        c. Produce a **tab-separated** text file named  
              /home/user/data/cleaned/event_type_frequency.tsv  
           that contains one line per distinct event_type, sorted by descending frequency.  
           • Do NOT include any header row in this TSV.  
           • Each line must have exactly two fields:  
                   event_type <TAB> count  
           • Lines must appear in this exact order and with these exact contents:  
                   view<TAB>6  
                   login<TAB>5  
                   purchase<TAB>4  
                   logout<TAB>3  
                   click<TAB>2  
                   unknown<TAB>1  
        d. Create a plain-text process log recording what you did, at  
              /home/user/data/cleaned/process.log  
           • The file can contain any number of lines you like, but it must be non-empty.  
           • Typical content would be the shell commands (or a short narrative) you used to derive the output file.  

    3.  Verification expectations  
        The automated checker will confirm all of the following:  
        • /home/user/data/cleaned exists.  
        • /home/user/data/cleaned/event_type_frequency.tsv exists and its contents are **exactly** the six lines listed above, byte-for-byte (UNIX newline at the end of each line).  
        • /home/user/data/cleaned/process.log exists and is not empty.  

    Hint: The quickest path is usually some combination of “tail -n +2”, “cut” or “awk” to extract the third column, then “sort | uniq -c”, followed by a second “sort -nr”.  Remember to reformat the output so the count becomes the second column and fields are tab-separated.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

    Path: /home/user/data/raw/events.csv  
    Exact contents (22 lines, each ends with a newline, no extra blank lines):

    id,user,event_type,timestamp
    1,user01,login,2023-01-01T08:01:00Z
    2,user02,view,2023-01-01T08:02:00Z
    3,user03,purchase,2023-01-01T08:03:00Z
    4,user04,logout,2023-01-01T08:04:00Z
    5,user05,view,2023-01-01T08:05:00Z
    6,user06,click,2023-01-01T08:06:00Z
    7,user07,view,2023-01-01T08:07:00Z
    8,user08,login,2023-01-01T08:08:00Z
    9,user09,purchase,2023-01-01T08:09:00Z
    10,user10,login,2023-01-01T08:10:00Z
    11,user11,purchase,2023-01-01T08:11:00Z
    12,user12,view,2023-01-01T08:12:00Z
    13,user13,logout,2023-01-01T08:13:00Z
    14,user14,click,2023-01-01T08:14:00Z
    15,user15,login,2023-01-01T08:15:00Z
    16,user16,purchase,2023-01-01T08:16:00Z
    17,user17,logout,2023-01-01T08:17:00Z
    18,user18,view,2023-01-01T08:18:00Z
    19,user19,login,2023-01-01T08:19:00Z
    20,user20,view,2023-01-01T08:20:00Z
    21,user21,unknown,2023-01-01T08:21:00Z

    Expected file that the agent must create:

    Path: /home/user/data/cleaned/event_type_frequency.tsv  
    Exact contents (each line terminated with '\n'):

    view\t6\n
    login\t5\n
    purchase\t4\n
    logout\t3\n
    click\t2\n
    unknown\t1\n

    The /home/user/data/cleaned/process.log file must exist and contain at least one byte.
